preamble: The shift from [a] to a glottal stop [ʔ] typically occurs as a form of glottalization. It is frequently seen at the beginning of words or as a way to resolve hiatus when [a] meets another vowel.

examples.note: Initial vowels are often preceded by a glottal stop for clarity or emphasis.
German consistently uses glottal stops before word-initial vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2014). A Course in Phonetics. https://archive.org/details/courseinphonetic00lade
Kohler, K. J. (1995). Einführung in die Phonetik des Deutschen. Erich Schmidt Verlag.
